title: NME Japanese Sales Figures
wikitext:
[]: NME Japanese Sales Figures             Quote Post by 1731298478 » Mon Dec 16, 2013 12:47 am Here's my attempt to work out/confirm NME's DVD/BD sales so far. I have used the latest entry I could find for each release. Some of the releases re-charted when 3.33 was first released. Note that this page has a lower figure for 1.01/1.11 (579,077) and a slightly different figure for 2.22 (838,929), so I may have made mistakes somewhere. 1.01/1.11 Sales (Total 600,107) Evangelion 1.01 DVD (Regular Edition): 62,332 ( Source - Dec 15, 2008) Evangelion 1.01 DVD (Special Edition): 303,916 ( Source [Post 393] - July 20, 2009) Evangelion 1.11 DVD: 51,924 ( Source [Post 155] - December 20, 2010) Evangelion 1.11 BD: 181,935 ( Source with correction - June 16, 2013) 2.22 Sales (Total 838,195) Evangelion 2.22 DVD: 373,820 ( Source - Dec 12, 2010) Evangelion 2.22 BD: 464,375 ( Source - May 19, 2013) 3.33 Sales (Total 581,107) Evangelion 3.33 DVD: 176,834 ( Source - December 8, 2013) Evangelion 3.33 BD: 404,273 ( Source - September 14, 2014)

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Dec 16, 2013 9:40 am Damn, Q did the worst out of the three. People outside Evageeks don't really like Q (not that I can blame them).

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Dec 16, 2013 9:48 am ^ Remember that Q's DVD and BD are only out since May, compared to the years since Jo and Ha's disc releases.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Dec 16, 2013 10:08 am Are those figures Japan only or international? - in which case Q has barely started outside Japan (and won't until next year in English-speaking countries).

[]:             Quote Post by 1731298478 » Mon Dec 16, 2013 10:14 am pwhodges wrote: Are those figures Japan only or international? - in which case Q has barely started outside Japan (and won't until next year in English-speaking countries).

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Dec 16, 2013 11:19 am TheFriskyIan wrote: Damn, Q did the worst out of the three. People outside Evageeks don't really like Q (not that I can blame them).

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Dec 16, 2013 11:21 am Strange though, since the theatrical release was the biggest success of the whole franchise, EoE included.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Dec 16, 2013 11:22 am It would be interesting to know the extent to which the older figures have been inflated by duplicate purchases from people trading up from DVD to BD.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Dec 16, 2013 11:44 am ElMariachi wrote: Strange though, since the theatrical release was the biggest success of the whole franchise, EoE included.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 16, 2013 11:49 am The bluRay sales have almost caught up to 2.0, give it a bit more time. That less people are buying DVDs should be less of a surprise.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Dec 16, 2013 11:57 am ElMariachi wrote: Strange though, since the theatrical release was the biggest success of the whole franchise, EoE included.

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Mon Dec 16, 2013 12:05 pm Interesting numbers, wished Q sold more but i'm not fretting. TheFriskyIan wrote: Damn, Q did the worst out of the three. People outside Evageeks don't really like Q (not that I can blame them).

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Dec 16, 2013 1:44 pm WunderBah wrote: Lets not pretend nearly everyone in Evageeks have resonated well with Q.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Mon Dec 16, 2013 1:50 pm Every time I see Q doing not so well, (when it comes to reviews, BD sales ect.) I want kick my pillow gently.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Mon Dec 16, 2013 1:51 pm It hasn't even been a year since Q came out on home video while the other two films had more than enough time go by to see an increase in their sales. It may have been the most polarizing of the theatrical films releases so far and the one with the most with mixed reception but really, I don't think sales say anything THAT much. Hell, Ha was probably the most "critically acclaimed" of them of all and it has had its fair share of detractors and not just on Eva Geeks, either.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Dec 16, 2013 3:03 pm Q's sales figures aren't that bad considering that Jo is only beating it by 30K units and has been out the longest and it didn't come with a film strip. Rei IV wrote: Ha was probably the most "critically acclaimed" of them of all

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Mon Dec 16, 2013 3:05 pm Jeez, how could I've forgotten that when I even replied in that topic! I had meant to say that out of the three films, Ha seemed to be the one with the most reception.

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Mon Dec 16, 2013 3:52 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: Oh I ain't pretending that cause I sure as Hell didn't resonate well with Q.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Dec 16, 2013 4:45 pm WunderBah wrote: Neither did Evageeks post-2.0 release apparently.

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Mon Dec 16, 2013 5:04 pm I meant the Dvds, sorry.

[]: NME Japanese Sales Figures             Quote Post by 1731298478 » Mon Dec 16, 2013 12:47 am Here's my attempt to work out/confirm NME's DVD/BD sales so far. I have used the latest entry I could find for each release. Some of the releases re-charted when 3.33 was first released. Note that this page has a lower figure for 1.01/1.11 (579,077) and a slightly different figure for 2.22 (838,929), so I may have made mistakes somewhere. 1.01/1.11 Sales (Total 600,107) Evangelion 1.01 DVD (Regular Edition): 62,332 ( Source - Dec 15, 2008) Evangelion 1.01 DVD (Special Edition): 303,916 ( Source [Post 393] - July 20, 2009) Evangelion 1.11 DVD: 51,924 ( Source [Post 155] - December 20, 2010) Evangelion 1.11 BD: 181,935 ( Source with correction - June 16, 2013) 2.22 Sales (Total 838,195) Evangelion 2.22 DVD: 373,820 ( Source - Dec 12, 2010) Evangelion 2.22 BD: 464,375 ( Source - May 19, 2013) 3.33 Sales (Total 581,107) Evangelion 3.33 DVD: 176,834 ( Source - December 8, 2013) Evangelion 3.33 BD: 404,273 ( Source - September 14, 2014)
